settings.title: The Morning Shift in the Hearthstone Kitchen
settings.excerpt: Long before the first guest stirs, the kitchen is already the warmest and busiest room in the house.
settings.body:
Guests see breakfast arrive looking calm. They do not see the hour before it.

Our cook Eleni is in by half six, baking the soda bread that fills the hall with its smell, and setting the eggs from the farm up the lane to come to room temperature so they poach properly.

Where the breakfast comes from

Almost everything on the table starts within a few miles of the house, which is the part Eleni cares about most:

  • Soda bread baked fresh in the kitchen each morning.
  • Eggs from the farm just up the lane.
  • Bacon from a butcher two villages over, and honey from our own hives.

The bacon comes from a butcher two villages over, and the honey is from hives we can see from the breakfast window. None of it travels far, and you can taste that.

The quiet before the room fills

There is a particular calm to the kitchen at that hour, before the first guest comes down. The radio is low, the oven is on, and Eleni works through her list without rushing. By the time you arrive rubbing your eyes, the hard part is done and the coffee is already on. That is exactly how we like it.
